item.title: One project was funded by the National Science Foundation.
item.description: Our project, “Advancing Speech Detection: A Hybrid Approach Using Large Language Models and Graph Neural Networks,” receives $400,000 in funding from the NSF. This initiative aims to improve the detection and moderation of hate speech in multilingual contexts to create safer online communities.

item.title: One project was funded by the National Science Foundation.
item.description: Our study, “Collaboration Patterns and Socio-Economic Impacts Analysis in Emerging Science and Technology using Machine Learning Algorithms,” aims to understand and forecast the societal and economic impacts of emerging Scientific and Technological (S&T) advancements driven by Leading Organizations (LOs) and their Contributing Partners (CPs). This research received $300,000 in funding from NSF.

item.title: One paper was accepted by ACL.
One paper was accepted by NAACL.
One paper was accepted by TKDD.
